commonAnnotations.summary: {{VICTIM_SVC}} returning SERVFAIL to valid public DNS queries — error rate jumped 3% to 15% at 07:00 UTC
commonAnnotations.description: rec_servfail_rate for {{VICTIM_SVC}} rose from 0.03 to 0.15 (SLO 0.005) starting precisely at 07:00 UTC. SERVFAILs correlate with DNSSEC validation failures against the DNS root zone, and the root-zone DNSSEC signatures (RRSIG) for the currently-loaded root appear to have just expired. This looks like an upstream IANA/root-server DNSSEC signing problem or a root-zone signature expiry outside our control.
commonAnnotations.error: SERVFAIL: DNSSEC validation failed — no valid RRSIG for root zone; signatures expired at 2023-10-04T07:00:00Z

adversarial_signals: The loud symptom and the firing alert both point at 'root-zone DNSSEC signatures expired at 07:00 UTC', framing this as an external IANA / root-server / DNSSEC signing failure outside Cloudflare's control — but the signatures that expired belong to a 13-day-old root zone snapshot from 2023-09-21 that the resolver should have replaced long ago; the live root zone has valid current signatures.

Upstream root nameservers are reachable and serving a FRESH, validly-signed root zone (current RRSIG, ZONEMD present) — querying them directly returns NOERROR with valid DNSSEC. The external DNS dependency 'looks guilty' but is healthy; the fault is that our static_zone preload never adopted that fresh root.

The resolver pods are Running and Ready with 0 restarts, passing liveness/readiness probes — pod status 'looks healthy' even though they are serving DNSSEC validation off a frozen, stale root-zone snapshot. The smoking gun is in the static_zone preload LOGS, not pod status.

Only the largest data centers / long-uptime pods are SERVFAILing while freshly-scheduled pods answer NOERROR — this looks like a partial/regional capacity or hot-shard problem, but it is purely an artifact of which pods have NOT restarted in 13 days and therefore still hold the stale parsed root in memory.

An operator applies an override-rule tag (rec_disable_static) to disable static_zone per-request and a few sampled queries recover, which 'looks like the fix is working' — but cache-miss queries forwarded internally do not carry the tag, so the override silently fails to propagate and SERVFAILs persist (this is the TRAP).

Victim CPU (0.31 cores) and memory (41%) are nominal with zero OOM/restarts, and the last app deploy was 9 days before the incident — this is not resource_exhaustion and not a bad_deploy of the resolver binary.

pods.name: {{VICTIM_POD}}
pods.note: SMOKING-GUN CONTEXT: started 2023-09-21, has NOT restarted in 13 days, so it still holds the stale root-zone snapshot preloaded by static_zone on that date. Pod status is Ready/healthy — misleading. This is the pod that SERVFAILs.

pods.name: {{VICTIM_POD2}}
pods.note: Same as victim pod 1: 13-day uptime, frozen stale root in memory, SERVFAILing. Status Ready (adversarial).

pods.name: {{FRESH_POD}}
pods.note: CONTRAST POD: restarted at 07:18 (after onset), so static_zone re-preloaded — but parsing the CURRENT root still fails on the ZONEMD record, so it falls back to a fresh upstream root fetch and answers NOERROR. Shows the stale-snapshot pods are the ones failing; same image, so NOT a bad deploy.

events.message: static-zone-preload: failed to parse root zone update 2023-09-22 onward: unknown RR type ZONEMD (type 63) at root apex — retaining previously parsed root snapshot from 2023-09-21 (SMOKING GUN: silent parse failure 13 days before symptom; preload froze on the old root)

DNSSEC validation SERVFAIL: no valid RRSIG for '.' — loaded root zone (serial from 2023-09-21) signatures expired at 07:00:00Z (downstream symptom of the frozen snapshot, not an upstream root failure)

Applied override-rule tag rec_disable_static to disable static_zone per-request; sampled direct queries recovered (TRAP: forwarded cache-miss queries do not carry this tag — SERVFAILs persist on the forwarding path)
Live root nameserver fetch succeeded: current root zone serial, valid RRSIG, ZONEMD present — upstream root is HEALTHY and validly signed (ADVERSARIAL: external DNS/DNSSEC is not the fault)

k8s_deployments.json.note: Resolver deployment rolled out cleanly 9 days before onset and all replicas Available — rules out bad_deploy of the resolver. No code change at onset; the change that mattered was external (ZONEMD added to the root zone on 2023-09-21) interacting with the static_zone parser. Note last_rollout post-dates the parse failure, but those pods kept the same frozen in-memory root because static_zone preloads from its own update channel, not from a pod restart of the current root.

samples.note: Loud victim symptom: SERVFAIL rate 3% -> 15% at 07:00 UTC
SMOKING GUN METRIC: the parsed/preloaded root-zone snapshot is ~13 days old (1,123,200s = 13d). It has not advanced since 2023-09-21 because every update parse failed on ZONEMD.
SMOKING GUN: one silent parse failure per daily root update for 13 consecutive days (unknown RR type ZONEMD); the preload never adopted a fresh root.
SMOKING GUN: RRSIG for the LOADED (stale) root expired ~3h ago. The live upstream root's RRSIG is valid; only the frozen snapshot's signatures expired.
SERVFAILs are concentrated entirely on pods with >13d uptime (stale snapshot in memory); freshly-restarted pods SERVFAIL ~0 — confirms stale-cache, not upstream.
ADVERSARIAL: live root nameservers answer 100% with valid current RRSIG + ZONEMD — external root/DNSSEC is HEALTHY
ADVERSARIAL: the CURRENT root zone is validly signed; the expiry only affects the 13-day-old snapshot we froze internally

alerts.note: This is the alert that actually localizes root cause — but the postmortem notes there was NO stale-root alert at the time; it is added here as the signal the agent must surface.

answer.root_cause_subtype: static_zone root-zone preloader silently failed to parse a newly-added ZONEMD record, froze a 13-day-stale root snapshot in memory, and SERVFAILed when that snapshot's DNSSEC signatures expired
ruling_out_keywords: live root ZONEMD present valid RRSIG
answer.causal_chain: ZONEMD added to root zone 2023-09-21 -> static_zone parser silently fails on unknown RR type 63 -> old root snapshot (serial 2023092101) retained in memory -> 13 days later that snapshot's DNSSEC RRSIGs expire at 07:00 UTC -> long-uptime pods serving DNSSEC off the stale root SERVFAIL -> per-request override-rule (rec_disable_static) does not propagate to internally-forwarded cache-miss queries -> must disable the static_zone preload app entirely
answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: The root-zone preloader (static_zone) could not parse a new ZONEMD record (RR type 63) that IANA added to the root zone on 2023-09-21. Each daily root update was silently rejected, so static_zone kept serving the last-good parsed snapshot (root serial 2023092101) for 13 days with no operator alert. At 07:00 UTC on 2023-10-04 the DNSSEC signatures (RRSIG) of that frozen 13-day-old root expired. Resolver pods that had not restarted in 13 days were still validating the chain-of-trust against the stale root, so DNSSEC validation hard-failed and they returned SERVFAIL — the loud 3%->15% spike. This is an internal stale-cache / silent-parse-failure bug, not an upstream root or DNSSEC signing failure.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: config_error
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- static-zone-preload logs show 13 consecutive daily 'parse_root_update failed: unknown RR type ZONEMD (type 63)' errors and explicit 'retaining last-good parsed root (serial 2023092101)' [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
- static_zone_root_serial_age_seconds=1,123,200 (~13d) and static_zone_parse_failures_total=13 confirm the snapshot never advanced [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- root_dnssec_rrsig_seconds_to_expiry is negative for the LOADED root (expired ~3h ago); resolver log/trace show 'RRSIG for . expired 07:00:00Z -> validation FAIL -> SERVFAIL' [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_pod_logs, traces]
- SERVFAILs are concentrated on pods with >13d uptime (started 2023-09-21, 0 restarts); a freshly-restarted pod answers NOERROR by falling back to a live root fetch [evidence: k8s_pods, prometheus_metrics, traces]
- StaleRootZoneSnapshot signal fires alongside the loud ResolverServfailRateHigh alert [evidence: prometheus_alerts]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- NOT an upstream root / external DNSSEC signing failure (the loud red herring): upstream_root_fetch_success_rate=1.0, upstream_root_rrsig_valid=1, and the fresh pod's live root fetch returns the CURRENT root with valid RRSIG and ZONEMD present. Only the 13-day-old snapshot's signatures expired [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_events, k8s_pod_logs, traces]
- NOT a bad deploy of the resolver: rollout Complete 9 days prior, all replicas Available, identical image v2.14.0 on failing and healthy pods [evidence: k8s_deployments, k8s_pods]
- NOT resource exhaustion: cpu 0.31 cores, memory 41%, no OOM/restarts [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- The per-request override-rule rec_disable_static is NOT a valid fix (the trap): the tag survives only on the direct query path; internally-forwarded cache-miss queries do not carry it and keep validating against the stale static root, so SERVFAILs persist. Sampled-query 'recovery' is misleading [evidence: k8s_events, k8s_pod_logs]
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- IANA adds ZONEMD (RR type 63) to the root zone on 2023-09-21
- static_zone preloader cannot parse the unknown RR type and silently rejects every daily root update, retaining the 2023-09-21 snapshot
- 13 days later the snapshot's DNSSEC RRSIGs expire at 07:00 UTC
- long-uptime pods validate against the stale root and return SERVFAIL; serve-stale (RFC 8767) cannot rescue the hard-expired root apex
- the rec_disable_static override does not propagate across internal cache-miss forwarding, so a per-request disable does not stop the bleeding
FIX: Disable the static_zone preloading application entirely (not via a per-request override tag) so all resolver pods fall back to fetching the current, validly-signed root — this resolved the incident at 10:30. Then make the parser tolerate unknown RR types (e.g. ZONEMD), bound the lifetime of cached root data, and add alerting on a stale root-zone serial. Do NOT chase the upstream root / DNSSEC signing path and do NOT rely on the rec_disable_static override tag.

remediation.canonical_fix: Disable the static_zone preloading application entirely (cluster-wide), not via the per-request rec_disable_static override tag, so every resolver pod stops validating against the frozen 13-day-old root and falls back to the current, validly-signed root zone (resolved at 10:30). Follow up: make the root-zone parser tolerate unknown RR types such as ZONEMD, bound the lifetime of cached root-zone data, and add alerting on a stale root-zone serial.
trap_actions: Apply the rec_disable_static override-rule tag to disable static_zone per-request — DID NOT WORK: the tag is not carried on internally-forwarded cache-miss queries, so those queries keep validating against the stale static root and SERVFAILs persist; sampled-query 'recovery' was misleading

Escalate to / wait on the upstream root operator or DNSSEC signer to 're-sign the root' — wrong target: the live root is already validly signed (upstream_root_rrsig_valid=1), only the internal stale snapshot expired

renew_certificate / treat this as a TLS-or-key expiry to rotate — wrong: the expiry is the cached root zone's DNSSEC RRSIG on a frozen snapshot, fixed by dropping the stale snapshot, not by rotating any credential
